name: Stickynote Workflow
description: Automated workflow: Stickynote Workflow. This workflow integrates 9 different services: stickyNote, httpRequest, formTrigger, wait, code. It contains 22 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.

nodes.name: Sticky Note

parameters.content:
LinkedIn Job Data Scraper to Google Sheets
Scrape live job posts from LinkedIn via Bright Data, clean them, and send to Google Sheets. Use for:
✅ Job Hunting — fresh, filtered roles
✅ Sales Prospecting — find companies hiring (aka growing)
⚙️ What's Used
n8n Nodes: Form → HTTP Request → Wait → If → Code → Google Sheets
External Tools:

Bright Data – Dataset API
Google Sheets – Template Copy

🔑 Setup – Credentials Needed

Bright Data API Key → Add to HTTP headers as: Authorization: Bearer YOUR_KEY
Google Sheets OAuth2 → Connect account in n8n

📝 Input Form – Fields
Used to define what job data to scrape.
FieldDescriptionExampleLocationCity/regionNew York, BerlinKeywordRole to searchCMO, Data AnalystCountry2-letter ISO codeUS, UK, DETime RangeHow recent the jobs should be"Past 24 hours" or "Last 7 days" (recommended)Job TypeFull-time / Part-time / Contract(Optional)ExperienceEntry, Mid, Senior(Optional)RemoteFilter by remote-friendly roles(Optional)CompanyFilter by specific employer(Optional)
🚀 Workflow Steps

User fills input form
Trigger snapshot via Bright Data Dataset API
Wait node + polling checks when data is ready (~1–3 mins)
Cleanup step:

Flattens nested fields (job_poster, base_salary, etc.)
Removes HTML from job descriptions


Send to Google Sheet

Sheet is pre-linked, 1 job per row
Example columns: job_title, company_name, location, salary_min, apply_link, job_description_plain


You use the data

Job seekers → Apply fast
Salespeople → Spot buyers & offer help



💡 Pro Tips

Use "Past 24 hours" or "Last 7 days" for fresher results
Leave filters empty if unsure — Bright Data will return broader results
Use cleaned data for:

Cold email personalization
LinkedIn outreach
Building ICP-based lead lists



🧪 Example API Body
json[ 
  { 
    "location": "New York", 
    "keyword": "Marketing Manager", 
    "country": "US", 
    "time_range": "Past 24 hours", 
    "job_type": "Part-time", 
    "experience_level": "", 
    "remote": "", 
    "company": "" 
  } 
]
📄 Template & Resources

📋 Google Sheet Template (Make a Copy)
📘 Bright Data API Reference

🛠️ Customize It

Add filters to HTTP Body (remote, experience_level, etc.)
Change polling interval if Bright Data is slow
Add custom logic to score/prioritize listings
Send filtered lists to CRM, Slack, etc.

This gives you a live stream of hiring signals — whether you're finding a job or pitching a service. One form. One click. Fully automated.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note1

parameters.content:
# 🔍 LinkedIn Jobs API – Parameter Guide

Use this object to query LinkedIn job listings.  
Each field lets you filter results based on different criteria.

```json
{
  "location": "{{ $json.Location }}",
  "keyword": "{{ $json.Keyword }}",
  "country": "{{ $json.Country }}",
  "time_range": "Past 24 hours",
  "job_type": "Part-time",
  "experience_level": "",
  "remote": "",
  "company": ""
}
```

## 🧾 Field Explanations & Valid Options

### 🗺️ location
City or region where the job is located.
Use a city or region name.
✅ Example: "Berlin", "New York"

### 🧠 keyword
Job title or search keywords.
Use terms like role names or skills.
✅ Example: "Data Scientist", "Growth Marketing"

### 🌍 country
Country code in ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format.
✅ Example: "US", "DE", "IL"

### ⏱️ time_range
Posting date filter.
Limits results based on how recently jobs were posted.
Accepted values:
- Any Time
- Past 24 hours
- Past Week
- Past Month
✅ Example: "Past Week"

### 💼 job_type
Type of employment.
Use a single value or comma-separated list.
Accepted values:
- Full-time
- Part-time
- Contract
- Temporary
- Internship
- Volunteer
- Other
✅ Example: "Full-time,Contract"

### 🎯 experience_level
Seniority level of the job.
Accepted values:
- Internship
- Entry level
- Associate
- Mid-Senior level
- Director
- Executive
✅ Example: "Mid-Senior level"

### 🌐 remote
Workplace type.
Accepted values:
- Remote
- On-site
- Hybrid
- (leave blank for no preference)
✅ Example: "Remote"

### 🏢 company
Filter by company name.
Optional. Use plain text.
✅ Example: "Google", "Spotify"

## ✅ Full Example

```json
{
  "location": "New York",
  "keyword": "UI Designer",
  "country": "US",
  "time_range": "Past Week",
  "job_type": "Full-time,Contract",
  "experience_level": "Mid-Senior level",
  "remote": "Hybrid",
  "company": "Spotify"
}
```

nodes.name: On form submission - Discover Jobs

parameters.formTitle: Linkedin High Intent Prospects And Job Post Hunt
parameters.formDescription:
This form lets you customize your job search / prospecting by choosing:

Location (city or region)

Job title or keywords

Country code

Filters like posting date, job type, experience level, and remote options

You can also optionally narrow results by company name.

🧠 Tip: Leave fields blank if you want broader results.

nodes.name: Sticky Note2

parameters.content:
🧠 Bright Data Trigger – Customize Your Job Query

This HTTP Request sends a POST call to Bright Data to start a new dataset snapshot based on your filters.

👋 If you don’t want to use the Form Trigger,
you can directly adjust the filters here in this node.

You can customize:

"location" → city, region, or keyword (e.g. "New York", "Remote")

"keyword" → job title or role (e.g. "CMO", "AI Engineer")

"country" → 2-letter country code (e.g. "US", "UK")

"time_range" → "Past 24 hours", "Last 7 days", etc.

"job_type", "experience_level", "remote", "company" → optional filters

📌 Tip:
Use "Past 24 hours" or "Last 7 days" for the freshest results.

# 🔍 LinkedIn Jobs API – Parameter Guide

Use this object to query LinkedIn job listings.  
Each field lets you filter results based on different criteria.

```json
{
  "location": "{{ $json.Location }}",
  "keyword": "{{ $json.Keyword }}",
  "country": "{{ $json.Country }}",
  "time_range": "Past 24 hours",
  "job_type": "Part-time",
  "experience_level": "",
  "remote": "",
  "company": ""
}
```

## 🧾 Field Explanations & Valid Options

### 🗺️ location
City or region where the job is located.
Use a city or region name.
✅ Example: "Berlin", "New York"

### 🧠 keyword
Job title or search keywords.
Use terms like role names or skills.
✅ Example: "Data Scientist", "Growth Marketing"

### 🌍 country
Country code in ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format.
✅ Example: "US", "DE", "IL"

### ⏱️ time_range
Posting date filter.
Limits results based on how recently jobs were posted.
Accepted values:
- Any Time
- Past 24 hours
- Past Week
- Past Month
✅ Example: "Past Week"

### 💼 job_type
Type of employment.
Use a single value or comma-separated list.
Accepted values:
- Full-time
- Part-time
- Contract
- Temporary
- Internship
- Volunteer
- Other
✅ Example: "Full-time,Contract"

### 🎯 experience_level
Seniority level of the job.
Accepted values:
- Internship
- Entry level
- Associate
- Mid-Senior level
- Director
- Executive
✅ Example: "Mid-Senior level"

### 🌐 remote
Workplace type.
Accepted values:
- Remote
- On-site
- Hybrid
- (leave blank for no preference)
✅ Example: "Remote"

### 🏢 company
Filter by company name.
Optional. Use plain text.
✅ Example: "Google", "Spotify"

## ✅ Full Example

```json
{
  "location": "New York",
  "keyword": "UI Designer",
  "country": "US",
  "time_range": "Past Week",
  "job_type": "Full-time,Contract",
  "experience_level": "Mid-Senior level",
  "remote": "Hybrid",
  "company": "Spotify"
}
```

In this workflow, I use Google Sheets to store the results. 

You can use my template to get started faster:

1. [Click on this link to get the template]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}
2. Make a copy of the Sheets
3. Add the URL to this node

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: google Sheets  O Auth2  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "On form submission - Discover Jobs".
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note9" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Snapshot Progress" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note10" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "HTTP Request- Post API call to Bright Data" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Wait - Polling Bright Data" node (n8n-nodes-base.wait) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "If - Checking status of Snapshot - if data is ready or not" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "HTTP Request - Getting data from Bright Data" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Google Sheets - Adding All Job Posts" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Edit Fields".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
